Implement the CodeRabbit Coding Plan for issue #2200: feat(app): Add Library section to left navigation (Templates + Profiles) ## Instructions 1. `cd ~/2025slideheroes-sophie` 2. `git fetch upstream && git checkout upstream/dev && git checkout -b sophie/issue-2200` 3. Read the full issue: `gh issue view 2200 --repo slideheroes/2025slideheroes` 4. Implement according to the Coding Plan below 5. Run `pnpm format:fix && pnpm lint:fix && pnpm typecheck` before committing 6. Commit with: `git commit -m "feat: feat(app): Add Library section to left navigation (Templates + Profiles) (#2200)"` 7. Push: `git push origin sophie/issue-2200` 8. Open PR via GraphQL (same-org fork — `gh pr create` doesn't work): ```bash gh api graphql -f query=' mutation CreatePR { createPullRequest(input: { repositoryId: "R_kgDON3X_Ow" baseRefName: "dev" headRefName: "sophie/issue-2200" headRepositoryId: "R_kgDORH2m4g" title: "feat: feat(app): Add Library section to left navigation (Templates + Profiles)" body: "Closes #2200\n\nImplemented per CodeRabbit Coding Plan." }) { pullRequest { url number } } }' ``` ## CodeRabbit Coding Plan ## Coding Plan ### Summary - Define the 5 curated templates as a typed configuration file following existing config patterns, since no template definitions currently exist in the codebase - Add Library navigation item to the personal account sidebar configuration with appropriate icon and positioning - Create a new Library page at `/home/library` using the established loader pattern for server-side data fetching of profiles and company briefs - Implement a tabbed interface with three sections (Templates, Individual Profiles, Company Profiles), each using responsive grid layouts with consistent card styling and Sheet-based detail views for drill-down
Design Choices
Design Choice 1: Where should the 5 curated template definitions live? **Options Considered:** 1. Create a new config file `apps/web/config/templates.config.ts` following the navigation config pattern 2. Store templates in the database with a new `templates` table 3. Define templates inline within the Library page components **Chosen Option:** 1 **Rationale:** Create a dedicated config file — this matches existing patterns (navigation, paths configs), keeps templates easily maintainable, avoids database overhead for static content, and allows TypeScript type safety.
Design Choice 2: How should the "Use this template" action work given template selection UI doesn't exist yet? **Options Considered:** 1. Omit the "Use this template" action entirely for now 2. Link to Generate step without any template context 3. Link to Generate step with a query parameter (`?template=xyz`) that prepares for future template selection integration **Chosen Option:** 3 **Rationale:** Use query parameter approach — this delivers the navigation path now while laying groundwork for the template selection UI integration. The Generate step can ignore the parameter until Step 4 is implemented.
💡 User Tips Regenerate the plan with different choices with `@coderabbitai `. ## Implementation Steps ### Phase 1: Template Data Definition & Navigation Update This phase establishes the foundational data structures for templates and integrates the Library into the existing navigation system. These changes are prerequisites for the Library page itself.
Task 1: Define Curated Templates Configuration Create the 5 curated template definitions as a typed configuration file that can be consumed by the Library page and eventually by the Generate step's template selection UI. - Create new file `apps/web/config/templates.config.ts` with template definitions - Define `Template` interface with fields: `id`, `name`, `description`, `colors` (array of hex values), `thumbnailUrl`, `previewUrl` - Export `CURATED_TEMPLATES` array containing 5 template objects with placeholder/sample data - Add Zod schema for runtime validation following the `NavigationConfigSchema` pattern - Use descriptive template names (e.g., "Professional Blue", "Warm Sunset", "Modern Minimal", "Bold Impact", "Nature Green")
Task 2: Add Library Navigation Item Update the personal account navigation configuration to include the Library menu item in the correct position. - Update `apps/web/config/personal-account-navigation.config.tsx` to add Library route - Import `Library` or `BookOpen` icon from `lucide-react` - Position the Library item in the "Application" section, after existing items but before any dividers - Set path to `/home/library` following existing path patterns - Add translation key `common:routes.library` for the label - Update `apps/web/config/paths.config.ts` to add `library` path definition in the personal account paths section
Task 3: Add Translation Keys Add internationalization support for the new Library section. - Update translation files to add `routes.library` key - Add keys for section tabs: `library.templates`, `library.profiles`, `library.companyProfiles` - Add empty state message keys for each section
🤖 Prompt for AI agents ``` This phase establishes the foundational data structures for the 5 curated templates and integrates the Library into the existing navigation system. **Template Configuration:** - Create `apps/web/config/templates.config.ts` with a `Template` interface containing: `id`, `name`, `description`, `colors` (array of hex values), `thumbnailUrl`, `previewUrl` - Export `CURATED_TEMPLATES` array with 5 template objects using descriptive names: "Professional Blue", "Warm Sunset", "Modern Minimal", "Bold Impact", "Nature Green" - Add Zod schema for runtime validation following the existing `NavigationConfigSchema` pattern - Use placeholder/sample data for thumbnails and preview URLs **Navigation Integration:** - Update `apps/web/config/personal-account-navigation.config.tsx` to add Library route - Import `Library` or `BookOpen` icon from `lucide-react` - Position in the "Application" section after existing items but before dividers - Set path to `/home/library` and use translation key `common:routes.library` - Update `apps/web/config/paths.config.ts` to add `library` path definition in personal account paths section **Internationalization:** - Add translation key `routes.library` for the navigation label - Add tab label keys: `library.templates`, `library.profiles`, `library.companyProfiles` - Add empty state message keys for each section ```
### Phase 2: Library Page Structure This phase creates the core Library page with its tabbed interface and establishes the data fetching patterns for profiles and company briefs.
Task 1: Create Library Page Route Set up the Library page file structure f
label: neo-issue-2200

task:
Implement the CodeRabbit Coding Plan for issue #2199: feat(storyboard): Add template selection UI to Generate step ## Instructions 1. `cd ~/2025slideheroes-sophie` 2. `git fetch upstream && git checkout upstream/dev && git checkout -b sophie/issue-2199` 3. Read the full issue: `gh issue view 2199 --repo slideheroes/2025slideheroes` 4. Implement according to the Coding Plan below 5. Run `pnpm format:fix && pnpm lint:fix && pnpm typecheck` before committing 6. Commit with: `git commit -m "feat: feat(storyboard): Add template selection UI to Generate step (#2199)"` 7. Push: `git push origin sophie/issue-2199` 8. Open PR via GraphQL (same-org fork — `gh pr create` doesn't work): ```bash gh api graphql -f query=' mutation CreatePR { createPullRequest(input: { repositoryId: "R_kgDON3X_Ow" baseRefName: "dev" headRefName: "sophie/issue-2199" headRepositoryId: "R_kgDORH2m4g" title: "feat: feat(storyboard): Add template selection UI to Generate step" body: "Closes #2199\n\nImplemented per CodeRabbit Coding Plan." }) { pullRequest { url number } } }' ``` ## CodeRabbit Coding Plan ## Coding Plan ### Summary - Create minimal template definitions (type + 5 templates) as a self-contained config since prior steps haven't delivered these - Build template picker using RadioGroup card pattern from existing PlanPicker component, wrapped in a dialog for action bar integration - Persist selection to existing `presentations.template_id` database field via new server action - Pass template ID through export flow and apply basic color theming to PPTX output, deferring full generator refactoring to Step 2
Design Choices
Design Choice 1: Template definitions do not exist from prior steps **Options Considered:** 1. Block this ticket until Step 3 is completed 2. Create minimal template definitions inline as part of this ticket **Chosen Option:** 2 **Rationale:** Create minimal template definitions as a self-contained config file, allowing the UI work to proceed while keeping template data easily updatable when Step 3 formalizes the structure.
Design Choice 2: TemplateConfig generator integration scope **Options Considered:** 1. Fully refactor pptx-generator to use TemplateConfig theming 2. Pass template ID to export action and apply basic theme properties where feasible **Chosen Option:** 2 **Rationale:** Pass template ID through the export flow and apply the template's primary colors/fonts to the existing generator. Full generator refactoring belongs to Step 2 (`#2197`), not this UI ticket.
💡 User Tips Regenerate the plan with different choices with `@coderabbitai `. ## Implementation Steps ### Phase 1: Template Definitions and Types Establish the template data model and create the 5 curated template definitions that the picker will display.
Task 1: Create Template Type Definitions Define the TypeScript interface and Zod schema for presentation templates, establishing the contract for template metadata. - Create new schema file at `apps/web/app/home/(user)/ai/_lib/schemas/presentation-template.schema.ts` - Define `PresentationTemplate` interface with fields: `id`, `name`, `description`, `colors` (array of hex strings), `thumbnailUrl`, and optional `fonts` configuration - Create corresponding Zod schema for runtime validation - Export a `TemplateId` union type for the 5 template IDs
Task 2: Define the 5 Curated Templates Create the template registry containing all template metadata for UI display and export configuration. - Create config file at `apps/web/app/home/(user)/ai/_lib/config/presentation-templates.config.ts` - Define 5 templates following the pattern: Consulting Classic, Modern Minimal, Creative Bold, Corporate Professional, and Tech Forward (or similar names aligned with design direction) - Each template includes: unique ID, display name, 1-line description, 5-6 color hex codes for palette preview, placeholder thumbnail path - Export a `getTemplateById()` utility function and `DEFAULT_TEMPLATE_ID` constant - Use placeholder thumbnail paths pointing to `public/images/templates/` (actual images can be added later)
🤖 Prompt for AI agents ``` Establish the template data model and create 5 curated template definitions. The goal is to provide the foundational types and data that the template picker UI will consume. **Create Template Type Definitions:** - Create schema file at `apps/web/app/home/(user)/ai/_lib/schemas/presentation-template.schema.ts` - Define `PresentationTemplate` interface with these fields: - `id`: string identifier - `name`: display name - `description`: 1-line description - `colors`: array of hex color strings - `thumbnailUrl`: string path to preview image - `fonts` (optional): font configuration object - Create corresponding Zod schema for runtime validation - Export a `TemplateId` union type containing the 5 template IDs **Create Template Registry:** - Create config file at `apps/web/app/home/(user)/ai/_lib/config/presentation-templates.config.ts` - Define 5 template objects with names like: Consulting Classic, Modern Minimal, Creative Bold, Corporate Professional, and Tech Forward - Each template must include: - Unique ID - Display name - 1-line description suitable for UI display - 5-6 hex color codes representing the palette - Placeholder thumbnail path pointing to `public/images/templates/` - Export `getTemplateById()` utility function that accepts a template ID and returns the template object - Export `DEFAULT_TEMPLATE_ID` constant pointing to one of the 5 templates ```
### Phase 2: Template Picker Component Build the template selection UI component following existing design patterns.
Task 1: Create Color Swatch Preview Component Build a small reusable component to display a template's color palette as visual swatches. - Create component at `apps/web/app/home/(user)/ai/[id]/storyboard/_components/template-color-swatches.tsx` - Accept `colors` array prop and render as small circular or rounded swatches in a horizontal row - Apply appropriate sizing (12-16px diameter) and spacing for compact display within cards - Add `data-testid` attribute for testing
Task 2: Create Template Picker Component Build the main template picker using the RadioGroup card selection pattern from PlanPicker. - Create component at `apps/web/app/home/(user)/ai/[id]/storyboard/_components/template-picker.tsx` - Use `RadioGroup` with `RadioGroupItemLabel` wrapper from `packages/ui/src/shadcn/radio-group.tsx` - Render responsive grid layout: `grid-cols-1 sm:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-3` with appropriate gap - Each card displays: template name, description text, `TemplateColorS
label: neo-issue-2199

task:
Implement the CodeRabbit Coding Plan for issue #2198: feat(export): Define 5 curated presentation templates ## Instructions 1. `cd ~/2025slideheroes-sophie` 2. `git fetch upstream && git checkout upstream/dev && git checkout -b sophie/issue-2198` 3. Read the full issue: `gh issue view 2198 --repo slideheroes/2025slideheroes` 4. Implement according to the Coding Plan below 5. Run `pnpm format:fix && pnpm lint:fix && pnpm typecheck` before committing 6. Commit with: `git commit -m "feat: feat(export): Define 5 curated presentation templates (#2198)"` 7. Push: `git push origin sophie/issue-2198` 8. Open PR via GraphQL (same-org fork — `gh pr create` doesn't work): ```bash gh api graphql -f query=' mutation CreatePR { createPullRequest(input: { repositoryId: "R_kgDON3X_Ow" baseRefName: "dev" headRefName: "sophie/issue-2198" headRepositoryId: "R_kgDORH2m4g" title: "feat: feat(export): Define 5 curated presentation templates" body: "Closes #2198\n\nImplemented per CodeRabbit Coding Plan." }) { pullRequest { url number } } }' ``` ## CodeRabbit Coding Plan ## Coding Plan ### Summary - Define a comprehensive `TemplateConfig` interface covering colors, typography, and density since the dependent PR `#2197` doesn't exist yet, basing the structure on pptx-generator's current hardcoded styling needs - Create 5 template configurations as individual files in a co-located templates directory, each using web-safe font stacks with proper fallbacks - Provide a central registry with helper functions for template access, following the pattern used by the AI Gateway prompt templates - Use static placeholder thumbnails for MVP preview images, with the structure ready for generated thumbnails later
Design Choices
Design Choice 1: TemplateConfig Interface Dependency (`#2197`) **Options Considered:** 1. Block this ticket until `#2197` is implemented separately 2. Define a minimal TemplateConfig interface as part of this ticket, treating it as a prerequisite step 3. Create a stub interface expecting it to be replaced later **Chosen Option:** 2 **Rationale:** Define the interface within this ticket since the template specifications provide clear requirements for colors, typography, and density properties that directly inform the interface structure.
Design Choice 2: Preview Thumbnail Generation **Options Considered:** 1. Generate thumbnails programmatically using html2canvas or similar 2. Use static placeholder images stored in public assets 3. Defer thumbnail generation to a follow-up ticket **Chosen Option:** 2 **Rationale:** Use static placeholder images for MVP since no image generation utilities exist in the codebase and the ticket explicitly allows "generated sample slide image" as acceptable, implying manual generation is fine initially.
Design Choice 3: Template File Location **Options Considered:** 1. `apps/web/app/home/(user)/ai/storyboard/_lib/templates/` - co-located with generator 2. `apps/web/config/templates/` - follows config pattern 3. `packages/templates/` - new shared package **Chosen Option:** 1 **Rationale:** Co-locate with storyboard module since templates are consumed by the pptx-generator in that directory and this follows the pattern of keeping related code together. A shared package can be extracted later if templates need cross-app reuse.
💡 User Tips Regenerate the plan with different choices with `@coderabbitai `. ## Implementation Steps ### Phase 1: Foundation — Define TemplateConfig Interface and Types Establish the type system that all templates must conform to, based on what the pptx-generator needs for styling (colors, fonts, sizes) and the template specifications in the ticket (tone, density, color palettes).
Task 1: Create TemplateConfig Interface and Supporting Types Define the core interface that captures all styling properties needed for presentation templates. - Create new types file at `apps/web/app/home/(user)/ai/storyboard/_lib/types/template.types.ts` - Define `TemplateColors` interface with properties: `primary`, `secondary`, `background`, `text`, `textMuted`, `accent`, and `chartPalette` (array of 5 hex colors) - Define `TemplateTypography` interface with properties: `headingFont`, `bodyFont`, `titleSize`, `headingSize`, `bodySize`, `captionSize` (sizes in points) - Define `TemplateDensity` as union type: `'compact' | 'balanced' | 'airy'` - Define main `TemplateConfig` interface combining: `id`, `name`, `description`, `colors`, `typography`, `density`, optional `previewUrl`, and optional `tags` array - Add Zod schema (`TemplateConfigSchema`) for runtime validation following the codebase pattern in `presentation-artifacts.ts` - Export all types from the existing `apps/web/app/home/(user)/ai/storyboard/_lib/types/index.ts` barrel file
Task 2: Set Up Templates Directory Structure Create the directory structure to house template definitions and supporting assets. - Create directory `apps/web/app/home/(user)/ai/storyboard/_lib/templates/` - Create subdirectory `apps/web/app/home/(user)/ai/storyboard/_lib/templates/definitions/` for individual template files - Create `apps/web/public/images/templates/` directory for preview thumbnail images - Add a README.md in the templates directory explaining the structure and how to add new templates
🤖 Prompt for AI agents ``` This phase establishes the type system for presentation templates, defining the `TemplateConfig` interface and supporting types that all templates must conform to. **Create Type Definitions:** - Create a new file `apps/web/app/home/(user)/ai/storyboard/_lib/types/template.types.ts` - Define the `TemplateColors` interface with properties for color theming: - `primary`, `secondary`, `background`, `text`, `textMuted`, `accent` (all hex color strings) - `chartPalette` as an array of exactly 5 hex color strings - Define the `TemplateTypography` interface with font and sizing properties: - `headingFont` and `bodyFont` as font stack strings - `titleSize`, `headingSize`, `bodySize`, `captionSize` as numbers (representing points) - Define `TemplateDensity` as a union type: `'compact' | 'balanced' | 'airy'` - Define the main `TemplateConfig` interface with: - Required properties: `id`, `name`, `description`, `colors` (TemplateColors), `typography` (TemplateTypography), `density` (TemplateDensity) - Optional properties: `previewUrl` (string), `tags` (string array) - Create a corresponding Zod schema `TemplateConfigSchema` for runtime validation, following the validation pattern used in `presentation-artifacts.ts` - Export all types and schemas from the barrel file `apps/web/app/home/(user)/ai/storyboard/_lib/types/index.ts` **Set Up Dire
label: neo-issue-2198

task:
Implement the CodeRabbit Coding Plan for issue #2197: feat(export): Refactor PPTX generator to accept TemplateConfig ## Instructions 1. `cd ~/2025slideheroes-sophie` 2. `git fetch upstream && git checkout upstream/dev && git checkout -b sophie/issue-2197` 3. Read the full issue: `gh issue view 2197 --repo slideheroes/2025slideheroes` 4. Implement according to the Coding Plan below 5. Run `pnpm format:fix && pnpm lint:fix && pnpm typecheck` before committing 6. Commit with: `git commit -m "feat: feat(export): Refactor PPTX generator to accept TemplateConfig (#2197)"` 7. Push: `git push origin sophie/issue-2197` 8. Open PR via GraphQL (same-org fork — `gh pr create` doesn't work): ```bash gh api graphql -f query=' mutation CreatePR { createPullRequest(input: { repositoryId: "R_kgDON3X_Ow" baseRefName: "dev" headRefName: "sophie/issue-2197" headRepositoryId: "R_kgDORH2m4g" title: "feat: feat(export): Refactor PPTX generator to accept TemplateConfig" body: "Closes #2197\n\nImplemented per CodeRabbit Coding Plan." }) { pullRequest { url number } } }' ``` ## CodeRabbit Coding Plan ## Coding Plan ### Summary - Derive `TemplateConfig` interface from actual hardcoded values in `pptx-generator.ts` since the vision document is unavailable - Extract all hardcoded styles (fonts, colors, sizes, positions, metadata) into a `DEFAULT_TEMPLATE` constant that produces identical output - Refactor `PptxGenerator` to accept optional `TemplateConfig`, falling back to default when not provided - Update the `powerpoint-actions.ts` wrapper to support template configuration while maintaining backward compatibility
Design Choices
Design Choice 1: The Template System Vision document (§4) referenced in the ticket does not exist in the codebase **Options Considered:** 1. Block on getting the vision document before proceeding 2. Derive TemplateConfig structure from the actual hardcoded values found in pptx-generator.ts **Chosen Option:** 2 **Rationale:** Derive the interface from actual hardcoded values, as this ensures the default template produces identical output and the structure can be adjusted when the vision doc becomes available.
Design Choice 2: The export action to update is unclear due to dual implementations **Options Considered:** 1. Update only powerpoint-actions.ts (the wrapper that actually calls PptxGenerator) 2. Update export-powerpoint.action.ts to use PptxGenerator class 3. Apply parallel template extraction to both implementations **Chosen Option:** 1 **Rationale:** Focus on powerpoint-actions.ts as it's the direct wrapper for PptxGenerator. The canonical export action may require separate migration work outside this ticket's scope.
💡 User Tips Regenerate the plan with different choices with `@coderabbitai `. ## Implementation Steps ### Phase 1: Define TemplateConfig Interface and Default Template This phase establishes the foundational type definitions and extracts all hardcoded values into a reusable default configuration. The interface structure is derived from the actual hardcoded values in `pptx-generator.ts` since the vision document is unavailable.
Task 1: Create TemplateConfig Type Definition Define a comprehensive TypeScript interface that captures all configurable style values currently hardcoded in the generator. - Create new file `apps/web/app/home/(user)/ai/storyboard/_lib/types/template-config.ts` - Define `TemplateConfigSchema` using Zod following existing patterns from `presentation-artifacts.ts` - Include `colors` section: primary, secondary, accent, background, text, error, chartPalette (array of hex colors), tableBorder - Include `typography` section: headingFont, bodyFont, sizes object (title, subtitle, heading, subheading, body, small), weights (normal, bold) - Include `layout` section: margins, positions by layout type (can reference structure from `layout-positions.ts`), slide dimensions, content padding - Include optional `charts` section: defaultColors, showLegend, legendPosition, showDataLabels, labelPosition - Include optional `assets` section: logo configuration - Include `metadata` section: subject, author strings - Export both the Zod schema and inferred TypeScript type
Task 2: Extract Default Template Configuration Create a default template file containing all current hardcoded values to ensure behavioral parity. - Create new file `apps/web/app/home/(user)/ai/storyboard/_lib/config/default-template.ts` - Extract font values: `"Arial"` as both heading and body font - Extract all font sizes: 40 (title layouts), 24 (default title), 18 (subheadline), 16 (text), 14 (subbullet), 12 (tables/errors) - Extract color values: `"FF0000"` (error), chart palette `["4472C4", "ED7D31", "FFC000", "5B9BD5", "70AD47"]`, `"666666"` (table border) - Extract layout positions from both inline code and `layout-positions.ts` constants, unifying into a single source of truth - Extract bullet styles: `"bullet"` for regular, `"circle"` for subbullets - Extract alignment rules by layout type - Extract metadata: `"Generated using SlideHeroes"` as subject, `"SlideHeroes AI"` as author - Export as `DEFAULT_TEMPLATE` constant that satisfies `TemplateConfig` type
Task 3: Add Template Config Index Export Create proper module exports for the new template configuration types. - Update `apps/web/app/home/(user)/ai/storyboard/_lib/types/index.ts` to export `TemplateConfig` and related types - Create `apps/web/app/home/(user)/ai/storyboard/_lib/config/index.ts` to export `DEFAULT_TEMPLATE` - Ensure exports follow existing barrel file patterns in the codebase
🤖 Prompt for AI agents ``` This phase creates the foundational type definitions and default template configuration by extracting all hardcoded values from the existing PptxGenerator implementation. **Task 1: Create TemplateConfig Type Definition** - Create new file `apps/web/app/home/(user)/ai/storyboard/_lib/types/template-config.ts` - Define `TemplateConfigSchema` using Zod (following patterns from `presentation-artifacts.ts`) - Structure the schema with these sections: - `colors`: primary, secondary, accent, background, text, error, chartPalette (array of hex color strings), tableBorder - `typography`: headingFont, bodyFont, sizes object (title, subtitle, heading, subheading, body, small), weights (normal, bold) - `layout`: margins, positions by layout type (reference structure from `layout-positions.ts`), slide dimensions, content padding - `charts` (optional): defaultColors, showLegend, legendPosition, showDataLabels, labelPosition - `assets` (optional): logo configuration - `metadata`: subject, author strings - Export both the Zod schema and the inferred TypeScript type **Task 2: Extract
label: neo-issue-2197
